schema.description: The representation of a contractor (individual or business) in Gusto.

uuid.description: The UUID of the contractor in Gusto.

company_uuid.description: The UUID of the company the contractor is employed by.

wage_type.description: The contractor's wage type, either "Fixed" or "Hourly".

is_active.description: The status of the contractor with the company.

version.description: The current version of the object. See the [versioning guide](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/idempotency) for information on how to use this field.

first_name.description: The contractor’s first name. This attribute is required for “Individual” contractors and will be ignored for “Business” contractors.

last_name.description: The contractor’s last name. This attribute is required for “Individual” contractors and will be ignored for “Business” contractors.

middle_initial.description: The contractor’s middle initial. This attribute is optional for “Individual” contractors and will be ignored for “Business” contractors.

business_name.description: The name of the contractor business. This attribute is required for “Business” contractors and will be ignored for “Individual” contractors.

ein.description: The Federal Employer Identification Number of the contractor business. This attribute is optional for “Business” contractors and will be ignored for “Individual” contractors.

has_ein.description: Whether company's Employer Identification Number (EIN) is present

email.description: The contractor’s email address. This attribute is optional for “Individual” contractors and will be ignored for “Business” contractors.

hourly_rate.description: The contractor’s hourly rate. This attribute is required if the wage_type is “Hourly”.

file_new_hire_report.description: The boolean flag indicating whether Gusto will file a new hire report for the contractor

work_state.description:
State where the contractor will be conducting the majority of their work for the company.
This value is used when generating the new hire report.

onboarded.description: The updated onboarding status for the contractor

has_ssn.description: Indicates whether the contractor has an SSN in Gusto.

department_uuid.description: The UUID of the department the contractor is under

department.description: The contractor's department in the company.

department_title.description: The title of the contractor's department.

upcoming_employment.description: The contractor's upcoming employment details, if a rehire is scheduled.

start_date.description: The start date of the upcoming employment.

setup_status.description: The setup status of the upcoming employment.

dismissal_cancellation_eligible.description: Whether the contractor's pending dismissal can be cancelled.

rehire_cancellation_eligible.description: Whether the contractor's pending rehire can be cancelled.

schema.title: Contractor

404.description:
Not Found

The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided UUID is valid.

schema.description:
Not Found 
  
The requested resource does not exist. Make sure the provided ID/UUID is valid.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.

schema.title: Not-Found-Error-Object

422.description:
Unprocessable Entity

This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

schema.description:
Unprocessable Entity
  
This may happen when the body of your request contains errors such as `invalid_attribute_value`, or the request fails due to an `invalid_operation`. See the [Errors Categories](https://docs.gusto.com/embedded-payroll/docs/error-categories) guide for more details.

error_key.description: Specifies where the error occurs. Typically this key identifies the attribute/parameter related to the error.

category.description: Specifies the type of error. The category provides error groupings and can be used to build custom error handling in your integration. If category is `nested_errors`, the object will contain a nested `errors` property with entity errors.

message.description: Provides details about the error - generally this message can be surfaced to an end user.

metadata.description: Contains relevant data to identify the resource in question when applicable. For example, to identify an entity `entity_type` and `entity_uuid` will be provided.

entity_type.description: Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.
Name of the entity that the error corresponds to.

errors.description: Will only exist if category is `nested_errors`. It is possible to have multiple levels of nested errors.
